heading: Sense of Congress
label: 7672 Sense of Congress
notes: Amendments2008—Subsec. (b). substituted “10 percent should be used” for “an effective distribution of such amounts would be—Pub. L. 110–293“(1) 55 percent of such amounts for treatment of individuals with HIV/AIDS;“(2) 15 percent of such amounts for palliative care of individuals with HIV/AIDS;“(3) 20 percent of such amounts for HIV/AIDS prevention consistent with , of which such amount at least 33 percent should be expended for abstinence-until-marriage programs; andsection 2151b–2(d) of this title“(4) 10 percent of such amounts”.
amendments: Amendments2008—Subsec. (b). substituted “10 percent should be used” for “an effective distribution of such amounts would be—Pub. L. 110–293“(1) 55 percent of such amounts for treatment of individuals with HIV/AIDS;“(2) 15 percent of such amounts for palliative care of individuals with HIV/AIDS;“(3) 20 percent of such amounts for HIV/AIDS prevention consistent with , of which such amount at least 33 percent should be expended for abstinence-until-marriage programs; andsection 2151b–2(d) of this title“(4) 10 percent of such amounts”.

children.heading: Increase in HIV/AIDS antiretroviral treatment
children.label: a Increase in HIV/AIDS antiretroviral treatment

children.text: by the end of fiscal year 2004, at least 500,000 individuals with HIV/AIDS are receiving antiretroviral treatment through United States assistance programs;
by the end of fiscal year 2005, at least 1,000,000 such individuals are receiving such treatment; and
by the end of fiscal year 2006, at least 2,000,000 such individuals are receiving such treatment.

children.heading: Effective distribution of HIV/AIDS funds
children.label: b Effective distribution of HIV/AIDS funds
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that, of the amounts appropriated pursuant to the authorization of appropriations under for HIV/AIDS assistance, 10 percent should be used for orphans and vulnerable children.section 7671 of this title
